scenarios.name: Mixed-classification run log, user picks the build-step finding
scenarios.description: Main path: a well-formed improve-bot run log with one finding of each major type. Exercises extraction of all five heading prefixes, classification into all four substantive tags, the fixed table, and the [build-step] pick producing an /improve-bot --self-improve-code invocation. Verifies only ONE finding is tagged [build-step].
scenarios.context:
User invokes /improve-bot-triage with no flags. The most recent file resolved by `ls -t documentation/soak-test-runs/improve-*.md | head -1` is `documentation/soak-test-runs/improve-20260430-0900.md` with the following contents:

---
# improve-bot run 20260430-0900

## Run summary
- T0: 2026-04-30 09:00:00
- flavor: --self-improve-code
- stop reason: wall-clock budget (2h) reached
- error count: 0
- games: 50

## Primary finding — Schema mismatch on Decisions tab
The producer writes claude_advisor fields but the UI dashboard for the Decisions tab expects state-machine fields. This is a concrete regression in the endpoint `src/orchestrator/decisions_api.py`; a ~10-line shim would fix it. broken since the v3 promotion.

## Secondary finding — PPO declining win rate
Observed cycle win rates dropping 0.5 -> 0.167 over 4 cycles. Candidate root causes: reward shaping over-penalizing expansions. This needs an offline investigation / experiment, not a code edit. hypothesis: the win_prob heuristic is leaking into reward.

## Tertiary finding — Phase 4.7 terminal sentinel holding
The terminal sentinel added in Phase 4.7 Step 3 shows 0 failed_games across 50+ training games (vs soak-4b 6/12). validated end-to-end; no action needed. HOLDING POSITIVE.

## Skill feedback — Wall-clock leak during interactive phases
The improve-bot daemon ran unsupervised for 4h+ while phase 1 and phase 2 talked to the operator. This is a skill bug in improve-bot itself — the meta-skill should add a phase 3 leak check.

## Suggested next actions
Review the Decisions tab shim and the reward-shaping investigation. Consider whether to pause evolve.
---

After the skill prints the pickable table, the user replies: `1`

scenarios.name: First-match precedence and ambiguous fallback, user picks the research finding
scenarios.description: Conditional/edge path: one finding contains keywords for BOTH rule 1 (research) and rule 4 (build-step) — must resolve to [research] by first-match-wins. A second finding matches no rule and must fall to [ambiguous]. User picks the research finding; output must propose a GH issue (not an /improve-bot invocation) and must NOT execute gh.

# improve-bot run 20260501-1400

## Run summary
- T0: 2026-05-01 14:00:00
- flavor: --self-improve-code
- stop reason: clean exit
- error count: 2
- games: 30

## Primary finding — Dashboard latency hypothesis
The dashboard tab renders slowly. We have a hypothesis that the endpoint in `src/orchestrator/api.py` is doing an N+1 query. This needs more investigation / a profiling experiment before we touch code. (Body intentionally contains both 'hypothesis'/'investigation' AND 'dashboard'/'endpoint'/a .py path.)

## Secondary finding — Operator mood
The operator seemed tired during the run. No clear keyword for any classification rule appears in this body. Just a note about session vibe.

After the skill prints the table, the user replies: `1`

scenarios.name: File present but using wrong headings — STOP with no-findings message
scenarios.description: STOP edge path: the resolved log exists but uses headings that do not match any recognized finding prefix, so fewer than 1 finding is extracted. The skill must STOP and print the 'no findings extracted' message rather than producing a table or fuzzy-matching the off-spec headings.

scenarios.name: Adversarial finding text with shell-significant chars + a duplicate finding, user picks the build-step finding
scenarios.description: Adversarial path: the [build-step] finding's title embeds shell-significant characters (a double-quote, a backtick, and a $) that, if interpolated raw into the emitted '/improve-bot --self-improve-code "..."' command, would break the quoting or allow shell interpretation (security: external log content must not become live shell syntax). The log ALSO contains a duplicate finding (two '## Secondary finding' sections with identical title+body) that must be deduplicated to a single table row. User picks the build-step finding, so the emitted command must be safe to paste verbatim.
scenarios.context:
User invokes /improve-bot-triage --file documentation/soak-test-runs/improve-20260503-1000.md. The file exists with these contents:

---
# improve-bot run 20260503-1000

## Run summary
- T0: 2026-05-03 10:00:00
- flavor: --self-improve-code
- stop reason: clean exit
- error count: 0
- games: 40

## Primary finding — Fix the "win$prob" `eval` regression in src/orchestrator/api.py
The producer broke the win$prob field; the dashboard endpoint regression is in `src/orchestrator/api.py`. A concrete fix is needed. Note the title intentionally contains a double-quote, a backtick, and a $ so the emitted command must escape or neutralize them.

## Secondary finding — Reward slope flat
The reward-trend slope is flat across the run. This needs an offline investigation / experiment to find candidate root causes. hypothesis: penalty saturation.

## Secondary finding — Reward slope flat
The reward-trend slope is flat across the run. This needs an offline investigation / experiment to find candidate root causes. hypothesis: penalty saturation.
